equities: down (large) - Broad equity drawdown led by transports, airlines, hotels, European and EM indices on oil-shock margin compression and risk-off flows; small-caps (IWM) underperform large-caps, with gold miners (GDX) the lone bright spot offsetting losses.

rates: mixed (large) - 10y UST rallies on flight-to-quality and the 10y-2y curve flattens, but credit gets crushed: HY, IG, airline, EM sovereign and European peripheral spreads all widen sharply while mortgage rates and 2y yields rise on stagflation pricing — long duration Treasuries (TLT/IEF) win, credit (LQD/HYG/EMB) loses.

commodities: up (large) - Gold rallies on combined safe-haven and inflation-hedge demand, supporting GLD and the broader DBC commodity basket; offset by EU carbon allowances (KRBN) declining on industrial demand destruction.

futures: mixed (moderate) - Fed funds futures (ZQ=F) reprice fewer cuts as breakevens rise, while airline-sector put-option demand spikes — net implication is tighter near-term policy expectations alongside elevated equity tail-risk hedging.

macro: down (large) - US household demand weakens via gasoline-price pass-through, falling confidence, rising unemployment expectations, slumping retail/auto sales, and a frozen LBO/IG primary market; EM central banks burn USD reserves to defend FX — broad stagflationary squeeze on consumer and credit cycles.

n1.label: Brent crude spot +30%
n1.description: Roughly 20% of global oil supply transits the Strait of Hormuz. A multi-day closure removes ~15-20 mb/d of seaborne flow, triggering an immediate spike in Brent and Dubai benchmarks as traders price scarcity and war risk premium.

evidence.note: Brent spot
WTI spot, narrower spread to Brent
Brent futures front month

n2.label: LNG / TTF natural gas +25%
n2.description: Qatar exports ~20% of global LNG, all of it through Hormuz. A closure halts Qatari LNG cargoes to Europe and Asia, forcing TTF and JKM benchmarks sharply higher as buyers bid for Atlantic basin and US Gulf cargoes.

evidence.note: Dutch TTF gas futures
Henry Hub, transmits via US LNG export pull

russia_invasion_of_ukraine_ttf_surge_7de.label: Russia invasion of Ukraine - TTF surge
russia_invasion_of_ukraine_ttf_surge_7de.description: Russia invasion of Ukraine - TTF surge

wti_crude_oil_price_spike_f7cc92.label: WTI crude oil price spike
wti_crude_oil_price_spike_f7cc92.description: WTI crude surges as the Russian invasion raises fears of supply disruption to global oil markets, compounding the existing energy shock from TTF.

evidence.note: WTI spiked over 12 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 11 days after invasion, a roughly 32 percent move.
Brent moved nearly 10 sigma higher in lockstep, confirming the shock was global crude not idiosyncratic.
Oil ETF tracked the futures move at 12 sigma, corroborating the price action.
Energy equities rallied nearly 6 sigma, consistent with sustained crude price expectations.

vix_spike_e709bc.label: VIX spike
vix_spike_e709bc.description: Equity volatility index surges as geopolitical uncertainty and commodity price shock raise tail-risk fears across global markets.

evidence.note: VIX rose nearly 3 sigma above pre-event mean, consistent with a tail-risk hedging surge.
Brent surged nearly 10 sigma, corroborating the broader commodity shock that raised growth and inflation uncertainty.
tool_error: empty series
European equities collapsed over 10 sigma, confirming the risk-off shock that typically lifts VIX.

usd_broad_trade_weighted_index_rise_364d.label: USD broad trade-weighted index rise
usd_broad_trade_weighted_index_rise_364d.description: The dollar strengthens on a broad trade-weighted basis as capital flees European energy risk and flows into the safe-haven dollar.

evidence.note: Broad trade-weighted USD rose 5.6 sigma above pre-event mean within 11 days, a tight quantitative move consistent with safe-haven flows.
EUR/USD fell 5 sigma, with EUR dropping from 1.134 to below 1.09, directly corroborating EUR-driven USD strength.
Dollar bullish ETF rose 5.75 sigma, confirming the broad USD move beyond just EUR.
Euro ETF fell 5.26 sigma, reinforcing the EUR-led USD index appreciation.

european_industrial_output_contraction_c.label: European industrial output contraction
european_industrial_output_contraction_c.description: Energy-intensive European manufacturers face input cost spikes and potential curtailment as TTF prices make production economically unviable.

evidence.note: insufficient window data

Global industrials ETF dropped 3.8 sigma, indicating market priced in significant industrial output contraction.
Germany equity ETF fell over 10 sigma, consistent with severe expected hit to industrial earnings and output.

us_airline_and_transport_equities_drop_9.label: US airline and transport equities drop
us_airline_and_transport_equities_drop_9.description: WTI spike sharply raises jet fuel and diesel costs for US carriers and truckers, compressing margins and triggering sector selloffs.

evidence.note: WTI surged over 12 sigma above pre-event mean, a massive supply shock to fuel costs
tool_error: empty dataframe (likely delisted, pre-inception, or no data in range)
Transport ETF dropped over 2 sigma, smaller move than airlines reflecting more diversified transport exposure
Airline ETF fell 5 sigma, peak coincident with WTI peak at day 11, consistent with fuel cost pass-through

us_gasoline_futures_surge_009a25.label: US gasoline futures surge
us_gasoline_futures_surge_009a25.description: RBOB gasoline futures track WTI higher as refinery crack spreads widen and retail pump prices spike to record levels in early March 2022.

evidence.note: WTI spiked roughly 29 dollars above pre-event mean, a 12 sigma move over 11 days, confirming the parent shock.
Retail regular gasoline rose 0.64 dollars per gallon, a 9.3 sigma move, showing rapid pass through to pump prices.
US gasoline ETF surged about 30 percent with a 9.6 sigma peak on the same 11 day horizon, tracking WTI tightly.
Crude ETF moved in lockstep with WTI, corroborating the parent magnitude.
WTI rose roughly 29 dollars above pre-event mean, a 12 sigma move, confirming the parent shock magnitude.
CPI energy index rose about 28 points from pre to post window, consistent with the gasoline pass-through though z is uninformative given monthly frequency.
US weekly retail regular gasoline jumped about 65 cents within 11 days, a 9 sigma move that is the direct pass-through channel from WTI to CPI energy.
Headline CPI rose 3.2 points across the window, directionally consistent with the energy contribution.

s_p_500_index_drawdown_3daf7a.label: S&P 500 index drawdown
s_p_500_index_drawdown_3daf7a.description: Broad US equity index sells off as elevated VIX signals higher required risk premia and margin calls force deleveraging across portfolios.

evidence.note: VIX rose nearly 3 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 11 days after invasion.
tool_error: empty series
SPY fell 2.4 sigma with peak drawdown on the same day as VIX peak, consistent with vol-driven de-risking.
QQQ fell 2.65 sigma synchronously, confirming broad equity sensitivity to the vol shock.

2y_ust_yield_rise_5c38be.label: 2y UST yield rise
2y_ust_yield_rise_5c38be.description: Short-duration Treasury yields rise as markets price in Fed rate hikes against an inflationary commodity shock, even as volatility surges.

evidence.note: VIX rose nearly 3 sigma confirming the volatility spike.
2y yield rose about 20bp, a modest 1 sigma move directionally consistent with the edge despite the vol spike.
5y5y breakevens jumped sharply, supporting the inflation channel that keeps short yields elevated.
Crude surge provides the inflationary impulse linking vol regime to higher short rates.
Short-duration Treasury ETF drifted lower, weakly corroborating higher 2y yields.

10y yields fell 2 sigma, consistent with broader safe-haven bid that includes gold

eur_usd_spot_rate_decline_6373f6.label: EUR/USD spot rate decline
eur_usd_spot_rate_decline_6373f6.description: The euro falls sharply against the dollar as European energy vulnerability and growth fears drive capital out of EUR and into the safe-haven USD.

evidence.note: EUR/USD fell roughly 5 sigma over the window, a sharp depreciation of the euro versus the dollar.
Broad trade-weighted dollar rose about 5.6 sigma, confirming the concurrent move with EUR/USD weakness.
Dollar bullish ETF rose about 5.75 sigma, consistent with broad dollar strength concentrated in the EUR cross.
Euro currency ETF declined about 5.3 sigma, corroborating the EUR/USD spot decline.

em_currency_basket_depreciation_0a23ed.label: EM currency basket depreciation
em_currency_basket_depreciation_0a23ed.description: Commodity-importing EM currencies such as TRY and INR weaken as a stronger dollar tightens external financing conditions and raises import costs.

evidence.note: USD broad index strengthened by over 5 sigma within 11 days, confirming a sharp safe-haven dollar bid.
INR depreciated against USD by over 7 sigma on the same horizon, directly consistent with the proposed channel.

european_industrial_equities_selloff_2ce.label: European industrial equities selloff
european_industrial_equities_selloff_2ce.description: Energy-intensive European manufacturers including steel, chemicals, and cement producers see equity prices collapse as margin compression and output curtailment risk are priced in.

evidence.note: Broad European equities fell over 7 sigma below pre-event mean, consistent with severe industrial earnings repricing

european_carbon_allowance_price_drop_ff4.label: European carbon allowance price drop

evidence.note: Monthly industrial production data too low frequency to register the shock in this window, so cannot directly confirm output contraction.

us_consumer_confidence_drop_9a5870.description: University of Michigan and Conference Board sentiment gauges fall sharply as record gasoline prices erode household purchasing power and forward-looking expectations.

wti_crude_oil_price_surge_2bee90.label: WTI crude oil price surge
wti_crude_oil_price_surge_2bee90.description: Crude oil rises sharply as Japan, the world's third-largest LNG and oil importer, signals emergency substitution of nuclear generation with oil-fired power plants.

Brent moved 2.85 sigma higher, confirming the move was global rather than a regional WTI-specific dislocation.
Crude ETF tracks the futures move at 2.4 sigma, corroborating the price action.
Energy equities rallied 2.3 sigma, consistent with sustained crude price strength.

wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.label: WTI crude oil price spike
wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.description: Strait of Hormuz closure blocks roughly 20 percent of global oil supply, sending WTI futures sharply higher as traders price in sustained supply disruption.

evidence.note: WTI spot jumped roughly 30 dollars from a 64.5 pre-event mean to a peak within 9 days, an extreme multi-sigma move consistent with acute supply shock
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at similar magnitude and shorter time-to-peak, confirming a global crude supply shock rather than a US-specific dislocation
Oil ETF tracked the futures move with a 22 percent jump, corroborating the front-month futures spike
Energy equities rallied moderately, consistent with higher crude prices flowing into producer margins

vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.label: VIX surge on geopolitical shock
vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.description: Equity implied volatility spikes as markets price in uncertainty over conflict escalation, oil-driven stagflation, and potential broader Middle East spillover.

evidence.note: VIX jumped over 10 points to a peak 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean within 6 days, a textbook geopolitical vol shock
Brent surged ~25 dollars, confirming the supply shock channel that fed equity vol
HY OAS widened 4 sigma, corroborating broad risk-off that drives equity vol higher
SPY fell ~3 sigma concurrent with the VIX spike, consistent with downside protection bid

hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.label: HY credit spread widening
hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.description: High yield OAS widens as oil price shock raises input cost fears, tightening financial conditions and increasing default risk perceptions for energy-importing leveraged issuers.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 9 days after the event at roughly 29 bps above baseline.
BBB IG OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming broad credit risk repricing rather than HY-only noise.
Brent jumped 21 sigma to roughly 95 dollars, providing the oil shock channel posited in the mechanism.
VIX spiked 6.7 sigma, consistent with risk-off conditions accompanying spread widening.
HY ETF sold off 8.7 sigma below pre-event mean, corroborating the spread widening signal in price space.

10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.label: 10y UST yield flight-to-quality bid
10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.description: Safe-haven demand for US Treasuries pulls the 10y yield lower as global investors rotate out of risk assets into sovereign bonds despite the inflationary oil shock.

evidence.note: 10y yield fell about 9bp at peak, less than one sigma, indicating a modest flight-to-quality bid muted by oil-driven inflation concerns
Sharp risk-off backdrop supports the flight-to-quality channel directionally
Massive oil shock created competing inflationary pressure that capped the rally in Treasuries
Long Treasury ETF rose mildly, consistent with weak duration bid

gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.label: Gasoline futures price spike
gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.description: RBOB gasoline futures surge as refiners and traders price in crude input cost increases and potential product shortages from disrupted Middle East supply routes.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars per barrel, a 20 sigma move, providing the input cost shock.
Brent corroborates the global crude shock that drives gasoline input costs.
Retail gasoline prices rose 0.59 dollars per gallon, a 19 sigma move, consistent with wholesale pass-through.
Gasoline ETF moved nearly 10 sigma higher, peaking on day 6 in lockstep with crude, confirming tight pass-through.

airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.label: Airline equities selloff
airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.description: Major US carriers fall sharply as jet fuel costs, a direct derivative of crude prices, compress operating margins on near-term unhedged capacity.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars above pre-event mean, a 20 sigma move confirming the supply shock magnitude.
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at 21 sigma, confirming a global crude shock that flows directly into jet fuel.
Airline ETF fell about 4.1 dollars (roughly 14 percent) at a -4.7 sigma deviation, with peak coincident with the crude peak at 6 days.

us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.label: US breakeven inflation rises
us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.description: 5y and 10y TIPS breakeven rates move higher as energy-driven CPI expectations are repriced, reflecting pass-through from crude into headline inflation.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars, an extreme supply-shock move providing the inflation impulse
5y breakeven rose about 10 bps, a clear above-2-sigma move in the expected direction
10y breakeven rose modestly by about 4 bps, directionally consistent but smaller as expected for a transitory supply shock
5y5y forward breakeven actually fell, indicating the market views the shock as front-loaded rather than persistent, which dampens overall sensitivity

s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.label: S&P 500 spot index selloff
s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.description: Broad US equity index declines as VIX spike reflects and amplifies forced de-risking by volatility-targeting and risk-parity funds liquidating equity exposure.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking at roughly 29.5 within 6 days, well past typical vol-targeting deleveraging thresholds.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma with peak drawdown coinciding exactly with VIX peak at day 6, consistent with mechanical equity liquidation tied to vol expansion.

2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.label: 2y UST yield rise on stagflation
2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.description: Short-end Treasury yields rise as markets price stagflationary oil shock alongside uncertainty about Fed response, creating a bear-flattening impulse at the front end.

evidence.note: 2y yield rose roughly 9bp with a moderate 1.7 sigma move, directionally consistent but not extreme.
10y minus 2y compressed by about 11bp, confirming the bear-flattening impulse at the front end.
5y5y forward inflation actually dipped slightly, weakening the inflation premium story and tempering sensitivity.
WTI surged about 30 dollars, providing the oil-driven inflation premium channel.
VIX rose more than 10 points, confirming the geopolitical shock parent.

equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.label: Equity put skew steepens
equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.description: Demand for downside protection in options markets surges as institutions rush to hedge tail risk, steepening the implied volatility skew on S&P 500 options.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, reaching levels consistent with aggressive hedging demand that typically coincides with skew steepening.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma, the kind of sharp drawdown that historically drives institutional put buying and steepens skew.
VXX moved 8.2 sigma higher, confirming a sharp repricing of forward volatility that supports a steeper put skew through dealer hedging dynamics.

leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.label: Leveraged loan price decline
leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.description: Floating-rate leveraged loans reprice lower as default risk perceptions rise for energy-importing issuers and loan mutual fund redemptions accelerate.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, confirming a sharp credit risk repricing during the window.
BBB IG OAS widened nearly 3 sigma, corroborating broad credit stress that pulls loans lower.

HY ETF fell over 8 sigma, showing severe credit selloff that historically co-moves tightly with loan prices.

ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.label: IG corporate spread widening
ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.description: Investment grade OAS widens in sympathy with HY as the oil shock raises input cost fears broadly and risk appetite deteriorates across the credit spectrum.

BBB IG OAS widened by 2.8 sigma simultaneously, strong directional co-movement with HY.
BBB-Treasury spread widened by 2 sigma, corroborating IG stress.
IG bond ETF declined modestly, consistent with spread widening but smaller magnitude than HYG.

10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.label: 10y-2y UST yield curve flattens
10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.description: The 10y yield falls on safe-haven demand while the 2y yield rises on stagflation and rate-path uncertainty, compressing or inverting the 10y-2y spread.

evidence.note: 10y yield dipped about 9bp, modest flight-to-quality bid but weaker than the short-end move.
2y yield rose 9bp post-event, consistent with short-end pricing in stagflation risk.
10y-2y spread compressed by nearly 2 sigma, falling from 0.66 to 0.57, directly confirming flattening.
Long-bond ETF ticked higher, weakly corroborating the safe-haven bid in duration.

us_consumer_gasoline_price_spike_40a293.label: US consumer gasoline price spike

WTI crude surged about 30 dollars, providing the upstream input cost shock that flows into RBOB and then pump prices.

trucking_and_logistics_equities_drop_18d.label: Trucking and logistics equities drop

us_airline_sector_put_options_spike_3f28.label: US airline sector put options spike

tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.label: TIPS real yield compression
tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.description: As nominal yields face flight-to-quality pressure while breakevens rise, the residual TIPS real yield compresses, reflecting stagflationary repricing of the real rate.

gold_price_rally_5560d9.label: Gold price rally
gold_price_rally_5560d9.description: Gold benefits from both the inflation hedge demand triggered by rising breakevens and concurrent safe-haven buying, pushing spot prices sharply higher.

russell_2000_underperformance_vs_spx_499.label: Russell 2000 underperformance vs SPX

us_israel_war_on_iran_strait_of_hormuz_c.label: US-Israel war on Iran / Strait of Hormuz closure

wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.label: WTI crude oil price spike
wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.description: Strait of Hormuz closure blocks roughly 20 percent of global oil supply, sending WTI futures sharply higher as traders price in sustained supply disruption.

evidence.note: WTI spot jumped roughly 30 dollars from a 64.5 pre-event mean to a peak within 9 days, an extreme multi-sigma move consistent with acute supply shock
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at similar magnitude and shorter time-to-peak, confirming a global crude supply shock rather than a US-specific dislocation
Oil ETF tracked the futures move with a 22 percent jump, corroborating the front-month futures spike
Energy equities rallied moderately, consistent with higher crude prices flowing into producer margins

vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.label: VIX surge on geopolitical shock
vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.description: Equity implied volatility spikes as markets price in uncertainty over conflict escalation, oil-driven stagflation, and potential broader Middle East spillover.

evidence.note: VIX jumped over 10 points to a peak 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean within 6 days, a textbook geopolitical vol shock
Brent surged ~25 dollars, confirming the supply shock channel that fed equity vol
HY OAS widened 4 sigma, corroborating broad risk-off that drives equity vol higher
SPY fell ~3 sigma concurrent with the VIX spike, consistent with downside protection bid

hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.label: HY credit spread widening
hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.description: High yield OAS widens as oil price shock raises input cost fears, tightening financial conditions and increasing default risk perceptions for energy-importing leveraged issuers.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 9 days after the event at roughly 29 bps above baseline.
BBB IG OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming broad credit risk repricing rather than HY-only noise.
Brent jumped 21 sigma to roughly 95 dollars, providing the oil shock channel posited in the mechanism.
VIX spiked 6.7 sigma, consistent with risk-off conditions accompanying spread widening.
HY ETF sold off 8.7 sigma below pre-event mean, corroborating the spread widening signal in price space.

10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.label: 10y UST yield flight-to-quality bid
10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.description: Safe-haven demand for US Treasuries pulls the 10y yield lower as global investors rotate out of risk assets into sovereign bonds despite the inflationary oil shock.

evidence.note: 10y yield fell about 9bp at peak, less than one sigma, indicating a modest flight-to-quality bid muted by oil-driven inflation concerns
Sharp risk-off backdrop supports the flight-to-quality channel directionally
Massive oil shock created competing inflationary pressure that capped the rally in Treasuries
Long Treasury ETF rose mildly, consistent with weak duration bid

gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.label: Gasoline futures price spike
gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.description: RBOB gasoline futures surge as refiners and traders price in crude input cost increases and potential product shortages from disrupted Middle East supply routes.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars per barrel, a 20 sigma move, providing the input cost shock.
Brent corroborates the global crude shock that drives gasoline input costs.
Retail gasoline prices rose 0.59 dollars per gallon, a 19 sigma move, consistent with wholesale pass-through.
Gasoline ETF moved nearly 10 sigma higher, peaking on day 6 in lockstep with crude, confirming tight pass-through.

airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.label: Airline equities selloff
airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.description: Major US carriers fall sharply as jet fuel costs, a direct derivative of crude prices, compress operating margins on near-term unhedged capacity.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars above pre-event mean, a 20 sigma move confirming the supply shock magnitude.
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at 21 sigma, confirming a global crude shock that flows directly into jet fuel.
Airline ETF fell about 4.1 dollars (roughly 14 percent) at a -4.7 sigma deviation, with peak coincident with the crude peak at 6 days.

us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.label: US breakeven inflation rises
us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.description: 5y and 10y TIPS breakeven rates move higher as energy-driven CPI expectations are repriced, reflecting pass-through from crude into headline inflation.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars, an extreme supply-shock move providing the inflation impulse
5y breakeven rose about 10 bps, a clear above-2-sigma move in the expected direction
10y breakeven rose modestly by about 4 bps, directionally consistent but smaller as expected for a transitory supply shock
5y5y forward breakeven actually fell, indicating the market views the shock as front-loaded rather than persistent, which dampens overall sensitivity

s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.label: S&P 500 spot index selloff
s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.description: Broad US equity index declines as VIX spike reflects and amplifies forced de-risking by volatility-targeting and risk-parity funds liquidating equity exposure.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking at roughly 29.5 within 6 days, well past typical vol-targeting deleveraging thresholds.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma with peak drawdown coinciding exactly with VIX peak at day 6, consistent with mechanical equity liquidation tied to vol expansion.

2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.label: 2y UST yield rise on stagflation
2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.description: Short-end Treasury yields rise as markets price stagflationary oil shock alongside uncertainty about Fed response, creating a bear-flattening impulse at the front end.

evidence.note: 2y yield rose roughly 9bp with a moderate 1.7 sigma move, directionally consistent but not extreme.
10y minus 2y compressed by about 11bp, confirming the bear-flattening impulse at the front end.
5y5y forward inflation actually dipped slightly, weakening the inflation premium story and tempering sensitivity.
WTI surged about 30 dollars, providing the oil-driven inflation premium channel.
VIX rose more than 10 points, confirming the geopolitical shock parent.

equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.label: Equity put skew steepens
equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.description: Demand for downside protection in options markets surges as institutions rush to hedge tail risk, steepening the implied volatility skew on S&P 500 options.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, reaching levels consistent with aggressive hedging demand that typically coincides with skew steepening.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma, the kind of sharp drawdown that historically drives institutional put buying and steepens skew.
VXX moved 8.2 sigma higher, confirming a sharp repricing of forward volatility that supports a steeper put skew through dealer hedging dynamics.

leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.label: Leveraged loan price decline
leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.description: Floating-rate leveraged loans reprice lower as default risk perceptions rise for energy-importing issuers and loan mutual fund redemptions accelerate.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, confirming a sharp credit risk repricing during the window.
BBB IG OAS widened nearly 3 sigma, corroborating broad credit stress that pulls loans lower.
Senior loan ETF fell 2.5 sigma within 2 days, directly evidencing leveraged loan price decline contemporaneous with HY widening.
HY ETF fell over 8 sigma, showing severe credit selloff that historically co-moves tightly with loan prices.

ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.label: IG corporate spread widening
ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.description: Investment grade OAS widens in sympathy with HY as the oil shock raises input cost fears broadly and risk appetite deteriorates across the credit spectrum.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened by nearly 4 sigma, confirming the parent move.
BBB IG OAS widened by 2.8 sigma simultaneously, strong directional co-movement with HY.
BBB-Treasury spread widened by 2 sigma, corroborating IG stress.
IG bond ETF declined modestly, consistent with spread widening but smaller magnitude than HYG.

10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.label: 10y-2y UST yield curve flattens
10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.description: The 10y yield falls on safe-haven demand while the 2y yield rises on stagflation and rate-path uncertainty, compressing or inverting the 10y-2y spread.

evidence.note: 10y yield dipped about 9bp, modest flight-to-quality bid but weaker than the short-end move.
2y yield rose 9bp post-event, consistent with short-end pricing in stagflation risk.
10y-2y spread compressed by nearly 2 sigma, falling from 0.66 to 0.57, directly confirming flattening.
Long-bond ETF ticked higher, weakly corroborating the safe-haven bid in duration.

us_consumer_gasoline_price_spike_40a293.label: US consumer gasoline price spike
us_consumer_gasoline_price_spike_40a293.description: Retail gasoline prices at the pump surge as RBOB futures gains pass through to wholesale and retail channels within days, squeezing household discretionary budgets.

evidence.note: US retail regular gasoline jumped roughly 0.59 dollars per gallon within 9 days of the event, a very large move consistent with rapid pass-through from futures.
CPI energy index rose about 30 points post-event, directionally consistent though sample is too thin for a meaningful z.
WTI crude surged about 30 dollars, providing the upstream input cost shock that flows into RBOB and then pump prices.
Gasoline futures ETF rallied about 17 points (roughly 17 percent of pre-event level) over 6 days, leading the retail move by a few days as expected.

trucking_and_logistics_equities_drop_18d.label: Trucking and logistics equities drop
trucking_and_logistics_equities_drop_18d.description: Carriers with fuel-cost exposure and thin margins sell off as gasoline and diesel input costs surge, compressing operating income forecasts.

evidence.note: WTI crude rose about 30 dollars over the window, confirming the underlying fuel cost channel
Retail gasoline jumped roughly 0.59 dollars within 9 days, a clear input cost shock for road carriers
Transport ETF fell 2.6 sigma, corroborating the trucking and logistics sell-off
Transportation ETF dropped 3.6 sigma with a 9 percent peak decline, consistent with margin compression from fuel costs

us_airline_sector_put_options_spike_3f28.label: US airline sector put options spike
us_airline_sector_put_options_spike_3f28.description: Implied volatility on airline equity options surges as traders buy downside protection against further margin deterioration, with put skew steepening sharply in XAL-linked names.

evidence.note: Broad equity vol spiked over 6 sigma, which typically co-moves with sector-level implied vol though does not isolate airline-specific skew.
Crude surge of 20 sigma is the upstream margin shock that motivates airline-specific hedging demand.
Airline ETF fell nearly 5 sigma over the window, the kind of move that mechanically lifts realized and implied vol on sector options.

fed_rate_cut_expectations_repriced_lower.label: Fed rate cut expectations repriced lower
fed_rate_cut_expectations_repriced_lower.description: Rising breakeven inflation reduces the market's conviction that the Fed can cut rates, pushing fed funds futures to price fewer cuts in the near-term easing cycle.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose roughly 10bp at peak, over 2 sigma, confirming inflation repricing
10y breakeven moved less, suggesting front-loaded inflation concern rather than long-run de-anchoring
2y yield rose about 9bp, consistent with hawkish Fed repricing alongside breakevens
Fed funds futures price moved against cuts at over 2 sigma, directly evidencing repriced cut expectations

tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.label: TIPS real yield compression
tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.description: As nominal yields face flight-to-quality pressure while breakevens rise, the residual TIPS real yield compresses, reflecting stagflationary repricing of the real rate.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose roughly 2 sigma, peaking at 10bp above pre-event mean within 6 days, confirming inflation expectation repricing
10y TIPS real yield compressed 7bp, weaker move but consistent direction
5y TIPS real yield fell about 1.6 sigma, an 8bp compression that mirrors the breakeven expansion
TIP ETF rallied 0.6 percent, corroborating real yield compression

gold_price_rally_5560d9.label: Gold price rally
gold_price_rally_5560d9.description: Gold benefits from both the inflation hedge demand triggered by rising breakevens and concurrent safe-haven buying, pushing spot prices sharply higher.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose by roughly 2 sigma within 6 days, confirming the parent inflation expectations move.
10y breakeven moved modestly higher, weaker than 5y but directionally aligned.
10y real yield fell about 1 sigma, the precise channel through which higher breakevens support gold.
GLD rallied roughly 1.7 sigma with time-to-peak matching the real yield decline, consistent with the proposed mechanism.

russell_2000_underperformance_vs_spx_499.label: Russell 2000 underperformance vs SPX
russell_2000_underperformance_vs_spx_499.description: Small-cap equities lag large-cap as energy-cost sensitivity and tighter credit conditions hit domestically focused firms harder during the selloff.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, tightening credit conditions for small-cap balance sheets that rely more on floating-rate and HY debt.
Massive oil spike raises input costs, hitting small domestic firms harder due to weaker pricing power.
Vol spike consistent with forced de-risking that typically punishes higher-beta small-caps more.
Small-caps fell 4.5 sigma, materially deeper than SPY's 3.16 sigma drop, confirming small-cap underperformance.
Large-cap selloff was real but shallower than IWM, giving a clear relative underperformance gap.

us_equity_sector_rotation_to_defensives_.label: US equity sector rotation to defensives
us_equity_sector_rotation_to_defensives_.description: Utilities, consumer staples, and healthcare outperform cyclicals as institutional funds rotate within equities to reduce beta exposure during forced de-risking.

evidence.note: Severe vol shock confirms forced de-risking conditions
Utilities outperformed with positive deviation, consistent with defensive rotation
Staples declined modestly, partially contradicting the defensive rotation thesis
Consumer discretionary cyclicals fell nearly 2 sigma, consistent with rotation away from beta

clo_equity_tranche_price_drop_c11b06.label: CLO equity tranche price drop
clo_equity_tranche_price_drop_c11b06.description: CLO equity tranches, which absorb first losses on leveraged loan pools, reprice sharply lower as underlying loan NAVs decline and default expectations rise for energy-importing issuers.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, raising default expectations on leveraged loan pools and compressing CLO equity excess spread.
BBB OAS widened almost 3 sigma, reflecting broader credit stress consistent with CLO mezzanine and equity repricing.
Senior loan ETF fell over 2.5 sigma within 2 days, indicating loan price stress that flows directly into CLO collateral pools.
High yield credit sold off over 8 sigma, signaling severe credit risk repricing that hits CLO equity hardest as first-loss tranche.

us_leveraged_buyout_pipeline_freezes_0dd.label: US leveraged buyout pipeline freezes
us_leveraged_buyout_pipeline_freezes_0dd.description: Leveraged finance underwriters pull or reprice pending LBO bridge commitments as loan market pricing deteriorates, reducing new deal volumes sharply during the window.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, pushing new-issue clearing spreads well above typical bridge commitment flex caps of 100-150bp.
BBB OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming stress extends through the capital structure and tightens underwriting risk appetite.
Senior loan ETF fell 2.5 sigma within 2 days, indicating loan market repricing severe enough to invalidate prior underwriting flex assumptions.
High yield ETF dropped over 8 sigma, signaling broad leveraged finance distribution channel dysfunction that historically halts LBO syndication.

usd_investment_grade_issuance_freeze_e12.label: USD investment grade issuance freeze
usd_investment_grade_issuance_freeze_e12.description: Primary market IG corporate bond issuance stalls as the OAS surge makes new deals prohibitively expensive for investment grade issuers, reducing supply but signaling acute credit stress.

evidence.note: IG BBB OAS widened nearly 3 sigma immediately on the event, consistent with primary market conditions deteriorating sharply for IG issuers.
Moody's Baa spread widened about 2 sigma, reinforcing that IG borrowing costs jumped above pre-event hurdle rates.
LQD declined modestly, a weaker confirmation that secondary IG prices fell, which historically correlates with primary issuance pauses but is not a direct measure of issuance volume.

usd_short_end_funding_stress_rises_c2e5e.label: USD short-end funding stress rises
usd_short_end_funding_stress_rises_c2e5e.description: Elevated 2y UST yield and stagflation uncertainty push SOFR-OIS spreads and FRA-OIS basis wider as banks reprice short-term dollar funding risk.

evidence.note: 2y yield rose roughly 1.7 sigma, consistent with front-end repricing pressure
SOFR ticked up about 1.8 sigma above pre-event mean while DFF was unchanged, implying a mild widening of SOFR over fed funds
Fed funds effective unchanged, so the SOFR move reflects funding pressure rather than policy
Curve flattened by about 11 bps, confirming the parent move
T-bill ETF fell about 1.5 sigma, consistent with higher front-end yields

edges.mechanism: Hormuz closure removes Gulf crude from global supply, driving WTI and Brent front-month futures sharply higher with rapid pass-through to oil ETFs.

supporting_data.note: WTI spot jumped roughly 30 dollars from a 64.5 pre-event mean to a peak within 9 days, an extreme multi-sigma move consistent with acute supply shock
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at similar magnitude and shorter time-to-peak, confirming a global crude supply shock rather than a US-specific dislocation
Oil ETF tracked the futures move with a 22 percent jump, corroborating the front-month futures spike
Energy equities rallied moderately, consistent with higher crude prices flowing into producer margins

edges.mechanism: Strait of Hormuz closure and war outbreak drove a flight to options-based hedging, with VIX spiking alongside oil and credit spreads while SPY sold off.

supporting_data.note: VIX jumped over 10 points to a peak 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean within 6 days, a textbook geopolitical vol shock
Brent surged ~25 dollars, confirming the supply shock channel that fed equity vol
HY OAS widened 4 sigma, corroborating broad risk-off that drives equity vol higher
SPY fell ~3 sigma concurrent with the VIX spike, consistent with downside protection bid

edges.mechanism: Oil price shock and geopolitical risk premium drive investors out of high yield credit, widening HY OAS as VIX spikes and HYG sells off.

supporting_data.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 9 days after the event at roughly 29 bps above baseline.
BBB IG OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming broad credit risk repricing rather than HY-only noise.
Brent jumped 21 sigma to roughly 95 dollars, providing the oil shock channel posited in the mechanism.
VIX spiked 6.7 sigma, consistent with risk-off conditions accompanying spread widening.
HY ETF sold off 8.7 sigma below pre-event mean, corroborating the spread widening signal in price space.

edges.mechanism: Risk-off bid for Treasuries pulls 10y yields modestly lower despite the inflationary oil shock, with the move partially offset by inflation repricing.

supporting_data.note: 10y yield fell about 9bp at peak, less than one sigma, indicating a modest flight-to-quality bid muted by oil-driven inflation concerns
Sharp risk-off backdrop supports the flight-to-quality channel directionally
Massive oil shock created competing inflationary pressure that capped the rally in Treasuries
Long Treasury ETF rose mildly, consistent with weak duration bid

edges.mechanism: WTI crude spike passes through to RBOB gasoline futures via refinery input costs and crack spread arbitrage, with retail gasoline following.

supporting_data.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars per barrel, a 20 sigma move, providing the input cost shock.
Brent corroborates the global crude shock that drives gasoline input costs.
Retail gasoline prices rose 0.59 dollars per gallon, a 19 sigma move, consistent with wholesale pass-through.
Gasoline ETF moved nearly 10 sigma higher, peaking on day 6 in lockstep with crude, confirming tight pass-through.

edges.mechanism: WTI and Brent spikes raise jet fuel costs, compressing airline margins on unhedged capacity and driving a sharp selloff in airline equities.

supporting_data.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars above pre-event mean, a 20 sigma move confirming the supply shock magnitude.
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at 21 sigma, confirming a global crude shock that flows directly into jet fuel.
Airline ETF fell about 4.1 dollars (roughly 14 percent) at a -4.7 sigma deviation, with peak coincident with the crude peak at 6 days.

edges.mechanism: WTI crude spike from Hormuz closure raises input costs and recession fears, widening HY OAS as default risk repricing accelerates across leveraged energy-importing issuers.

supporting_data.note: HY OAS widened nearly 30 bps with a near 4 sigma move, peaking 9 days after the event, directly confirming spread widening.
WTI spiked over 30 dollars at 20 sigma, an extreme supply shock providing the upstream driver.
BBB IG spreads also widened nearly 3 sigma, showing credit stress extends into IG and supports the broader credit channel.
VIX jumped 10 points at 6.7 sigma, confirming a generalized risk-off backdrop consistent with HY widening.
HY ETF fell over 8 sigma, corroborating credit weakness via price action.

edges.mechanism: WTI crude spike lifts near-term inflation expectations, with 5y breakevens repricing more than 10y as the shock is seen as transitory.

supporting_data.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars, an extreme supply-shock move providing the inflation impulse
5y breakeven rose about 10 bps, a clear above-2-sigma move in the expected direction
10y breakeven rose modestly by about 4 bps, directionally consistent but smaller as expected for a transitory supply shock
5y5y forward breakeven actually fell, indicating the market views the shock as front-loaded rather than persistent, which dampens overall sensitivity

edges.mechanism: WTI crude spike from Hormuz closure injects an inflation and growth shock that amplifies equity implied volatility, with VIX rising in lockstep as oil disruption fears compound geopolitical uncertainty.

supporting_data.note: WTI moved roughly 20 sigma above pre-event mean, a 47 percent spike consistent with a major supply shock
VIX surged 6.7 sigma to peak around 29.5, with time-to-peak of 6 days closely tracking the oil move
Oil ETF confirms the WTI move with similar timing, ruling out a single-series artifact
Equity index fell 3.2 sigma over the same window, consistent with the risk-off channel that drives VIX higher

edges.mechanism: VIX surge reflects broad risk repricing that simultaneously drives institutional demand for higher compensation on sub-investment-grade debt, widening HY OAS in lockstep with equity vol.

supporting_data.note: VIX spiked over 6 sigma with peak at day 6, confirming a sharp risk-off shock.
HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma to peak at day 9, closely tracking the VIX surge with expected lag.
BBB IG spreads also widened almost 3 sigma, corroborating broad credit repricing consistent with the vol shock.
HYG ETF fell sharply (over 8 sigma) confirming HY price action consistent with material spread widening.

edges.mechanism: VIX spike produced only a brief, shallow flight-to-quality bid in 10y Treasuries, with the inflationary oil shock dominating and pushing the front end higher.

supporting_data.note: Large vol spike confirms the geopolitical risk-off shock
10y yield dipped only modestly and briefly, peak deviation under 9bp and post-event mean nearly flat to pre-event
2y yield actually rose, suggesting stagflation pricing offset flight-to-quality
Long-bond ETF barely moved, inconsistent with a strong duration bid
Equities sold off sharply, confirming risk-off but without commensurate Treasury rally

edges.mechanism: VIX spike beyond systematic trigger levels forces mechanical de-risking by vol-targeting and risk-parity funds, amplifying the S&P 500 drawdown alongside fundamental risk repricing.

supporting_data.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking at roughly 29.5 within 6 days, well past typical vol-targeting deleveraging thresholds.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma with peak drawdown coinciding exactly with VIX peak at day 6, consistent with mechanical equity liquidation tied to vol expansion.

edges.mechanism: Oil shock embedded in the VIX surge lifts near-term inflation expectations, pushing 2y yields up while the curve bear-flattens as longer maturities price growth risk.

supporting_data.note: 2y yield rose roughly 9bp with a moderate 1.7 sigma move, directionally consistent but not extreme.
10y minus 2y compressed by about 11bp, confirming the bear-flattening impulse at the front end.
5y5y forward inflation actually dipped slightly, weakening the inflation premium story and tempering sensitivity.
WTI surged about 30 dollars, providing the oil-driven inflation premium channel.
VIX rose more than 10 points, confirming the geopolitical shock parent.

edges.mechanism: VIX spike reflects surging demand for downside protection, steepening put skew as dealers raise premiums for out-of-the-money puts during the geopolitical shock.

supporting_data.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, reaching levels consistent with aggressive hedging demand that typically coincides with skew steepening.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma, the kind of sharp drawdown that historically drives institutional put buying and steepens skew.
VXX moved 8.2 sigma higher, confirming a sharp repricing of forward volatility that supports a steeper put skew through dealer hedging dynamics.

edges.mechanism: HY spread widening reflects tightening financial conditions and rising default risk, expanding equity risk premia and pulling the S&P 500 lower with HYG and SPY moving in lockstep.

supporting_data.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, a clear tightening of credit conditions
IG BBB spreads also widened nearly 3 sigma, corroborating broad credit risk repricing
SPY declined over 3 sigma in the same window, consistent direction with HY weakness
HY ETF sold off over 8 sigma, confirming the credit stress channel in tradable terms

edges.mechanism: HY OAS widening spills into the leveraged loan market as credit risk repricing and ETF/fund redemption pressure force loan bids lower.

edges.mechanism: HY spread widening pulls IG spreads wider through cross-credit contagion as risk appetite deteriorates, though IG moves are more muted given higher quality buffer.

edges.mechanism: Long-end yields drift lower on safe-haven demand while short-end yields rise on inflation and rate-path uncertainty, compressing the 10y-2y spread.

edges.mechanism: RBOB futures gains pass through to wholesale and retail gasoline within days via refiner and retailer margin mechanics, lifting pump prices and CPI energy.

edges.mechanism: Gasoline and crude price spikes lift near-term inflation expectations, with 5y breakevens rising more than 10y as the shock is priced as transitory.

edges.mechanism: Surging gasoline and crude prices raise diesel and fuel expense assumptions for trucking firms, compressing margin forecasts and driving transportation equities lower.

edges.mechanism: Sharp airline equity drawdown alongside a broad volatility spike drives institutional demand for airline puts, lifting sector implied vol and steepening put skew.

vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.label: VIX surge on geopolitical shock

hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.label: HY credit spread widening

case_studies.triggering_event: US-Israel war on Iran / Strait of Hormuz closure

us_israel_war_on_iran_strait_of_hormuz_c.label: US-Israel war on Iran / Strait of Hormuz closure
us_israel_war_on_iran_strait_of_hormuz_c.description: US-Israel war on Iran / Strait of Hormuz closure

wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.label: WTI crude oil price spike
wti_crude_oil_price_spike_22e8eb.description: Strait of Hormuz closure blocks roughly 20 percent of global oil supply, sending WTI futures sharply higher as traders price in sustained supply disruption.

evidence.note: WTI spot jumped roughly 30 dollars from a 64.5 pre-event mean to a peak within 9 days, an extreme multi-sigma move consistent with acute supply shock
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at similar magnitude and shorter time-to-peak, confirming a global crude supply shock rather than a US-specific dislocation
Oil ETF tracked the futures move with a 22 percent jump, corroborating the front-month futures spike
Energy equities rallied moderately, consistent with higher crude prices flowing into producer margins

vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.label: VIX surge on geopolitical shock
vix_surge_on_geopolitical_shock_06cd63.description: Equity implied volatility spikes as markets price in uncertainty over conflict escalation, oil-driven stagflation, and potential broader Middle East spillover.

evidence.note: VIX jumped over 10 points to a peak 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean within 6 days, a textbook geopolitical vol shock
Brent surged ~25 dollars, confirming the supply shock channel that fed equity vol
HY OAS widened 4 sigma, corroborating broad risk-off that drives equity vol higher
SPY fell ~3 sigma concurrent with the VIX spike, consistent with downside protection bid

hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.label: HY credit spread widening
hy_credit_spread_widening_5eee15.description: High yield OAS widens as oil price shock raises input cost fears, tightening financial conditions and increasing default risk perceptions for energy-importing leveraged issuers.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking 9 days after the event at roughly 29 bps above baseline.
BBB IG OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming broad credit risk repricing rather than HY-only noise.
Brent jumped 21 sigma to roughly 95 dollars, providing the oil shock channel posited in the mechanism.
VIX spiked 6.7 sigma, consistent with risk-off conditions accompanying spread widening.
HY ETF sold off 8.7 sigma below pre-event mean, corroborating the spread widening signal in price space.

10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.label: 10y UST yield flight-to-quality bid
10y_ust_yield_flight_to_quality_bid_a6af.description: Safe-haven demand for US Treasuries pulls the 10y yield lower as global investors rotate out of risk assets into sovereign bonds despite the inflationary oil shock.

evidence.note: 10y yield fell about 9bp at peak, less than one sigma, indicating a modest flight-to-quality bid muted by oil-driven inflation concerns
Sharp risk-off backdrop supports the flight-to-quality channel directionally
Massive oil shock created competing inflationary pressure that capped the rally in Treasuries
Long Treasury ETF rose mildly, consistent with weak duration bid

gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.label: Gasoline futures price spike
gasoline_futures_price_spike_fc7abf.description: RBOB gasoline futures surge as refiners and traders price in crude input cost increases and potential product shortages from disrupted Middle East supply routes.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars per barrel, a 20 sigma move, providing the input cost shock.
Brent corroborates the global crude shock that drives gasoline input costs.
Retail gasoline prices rose 0.59 dollars per gallon, a 19 sigma move, consistent with wholesale pass-through.
Gasoline ETF moved nearly 10 sigma higher, peaking on day 6 in lockstep with crude, confirming tight pass-through.

airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.label: Airline equities selloff
airline_equities_selloff_e9b4b4.description: Major US carriers fall sharply as jet fuel costs, a direct derivative of crude prices, compress operating margins on near-term unhedged capacity.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars above pre-event mean, a 20 sigma move confirming the supply shock magnitude.
Brent moved in lockstep with WTI at 21 sigma, confirming a global crude shock that flows directly into jet fuel.
Airline ETF fell about 4.1 dollars (roughly 14 percent) at a -4.7 sigma deviation, with peak coincident with the crude peak at 6 days.

us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.label: US breakeven inflation rises
us_breakeven_inflation_rises_8ba355.description: 5y and 10y TIPS breakeven rates move higher as energy-driven CPI expectations are repriced, reflecting pass-through from crude into headline inflation.

evidence.note: WTI surged roughly 30 dollars, an extreme supply-shock move providing the inflation impulse
5y breakeven rose about 10 bps, a clear above-2-sigma move in the expected direction
10y breakeven rose modestly by about 4 bps, directionally consistent but smaller as expected for a transitory supply shock
5y5y forward breakeven actually fell, indicating the market views the shock as front-loaded rather than persistent, which dampens overall sensitivity

s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.label: S&P 500 spot index selloff
s_p_500_spot_index_selloff_a9adc8.description: Broad US equity index declines as VIX spike reflects and amplifies forced de-risking by volatility-targeting and risk-parity funds liquidating equity exposure.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, peaking at roughly 29.5 within 6 days, well past typical vol-targeting deleveraging thresholds.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma with peak drawdown coinciding exactly with VIX peak at day 6, consistent with mechanical equity liquidation tied to vol expansion.

2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.label: 2y UST yield rise on stagflation
2y_ust_yield_rise_on_stagflation_174aec.description: Short-end Treasury yields rise as markets price stagflationary oil shock alongside uncertainty about Fed response, creating a bear-flattening impulse at the front end.

evidence.note: 2y yield rose roughly 9bp with a moderate 1.7 sigma move, directionally consistent but not extreme.
10y minus 2y compressed by about 11bp, confirming the bear-flattening impulse at the front end.
5y5y forward inflation actually dipped slightly, weakening the inflation premium story and tempering sensitivity.
WTI surged about 30 dollars, providing the oil-driven inflation premium channel.
VIX rose more than 10 points, confirming the geopolitical shock parent.

equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.label: Equity put skew steepens
equity_put_skew_steepens_552c13.description: Demand for downside protection in options markets surges as institutions rush to hedge tail risk, steepening the implied volatility skew on S&P 500 options.

evidence.note: VIX surged 6.7 sigma above pre-event mean, reaching levels consistent with aggressive hedging demand that typically coincides with skew steepening.
SPY fell 3.2 sigma, the kind of sharp drawdown that historically drives institutional put buying and steepens skew.
VXX moved 8.2 sigma higher, confirming a sharp repricing of forward volatility that supports a steeper put skew through dealer hedging dynamics.

leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.label: Leveraged loan price decline
leveraged_loan_price_decline_879157.description: Floating-rate leveraged loans reprice lower as default risk perceptions rise for energy-importing issuers and loan mutual fund redemptions accelerate.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, confirming a sharp credit risk repricing during the window.
BBB IG OAS widened nearly 3 sigma, corroborating broad credit stress that pulls loans lower.
Senior loan ETF fell 2.5 sigma within 2 days, directly evidencing leveraged loan price decline contemporaneous with HY widening.
HY ETF fell over 8 sigma, showing severe credit selloff that historically co-moves tightly with loan prices.

ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.label: IG corporate spread widening
ig_corporate_spread_widening_547504.description: Investment grade OAS widens in sympathy with HY as the oil shock raises input cost fears broadly and risk appetite deteriorates across the credit spectrum.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened by nearly 4 sigma, confirming the parent move.
BBB IG OAS widened by 2.8 sigma simultaneously, strong directional co-movement with HY.
BBB-Treasury spread widened by 2 sigma, corroborating IG stress.
IG bond ETF declined modestly, consistent with spread widening but smaller magnitude than HYG.

10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.label: 10y-2y UST yield curve flattens
10y_2y_ust_yield_curve_flattens_31e9ba.description: The 10y yield falls on safe-haven demand while the 2y yield rises on stagflation and rate-path uncertainty, compressing or inverting the 10y-2y spread.

evidence.note: 10y yield dipped about 9bp, modest flight-to-quality bid but weaker than the short-end move.
2y yield rose 9bp post-event, consistent with short-end pricing in stagflation risk.
10y-2y spread compressed by nearly 2 sigma, falling from 0.66 to 0.57, directly confirming flattening.
Long-bond ETF ticked higher, weakly corroborating the safe-haven bid in duration.

us_consumer_gasoline_price_spike_40a293.label: US consumer gasoline price spike
us_consumer_gasoline_price_spike_40a293.description: Retail gasoline prices at the pump surge as RBOB futures gains pass through to wholesale and retail channels within days, squeezing household discretionary budgets.

evidence.note: US retail regular gasoline jumped roughly 0.59 dollars per gallon within 9 days of the event, a very large move consistent with rapid pass-through from futures.
CPI energy index rose about 30 points post-event, directionally consistent though sample is too thin for a meaningful z.
WTI crude surged about 30 dollars, providing the upstream input cost shock that flows into RBOB and then pump prices.
Gasoline futures ETF rallied about 17 points (roughly 17 percent of pre-event level) over 6 days, leading the retail move by a few days as expected.

trucking_and_logistics_equities_drop_18d.label: Trucking and logistics equities drop
trucking_and_logistics_equities_drop_18d.description: Carriers with fuel-cost exposure and thin margins sell off as gasoline and diesel input costs surge, compressing operating income forecasts.

evidence.note: WTI crude rose about 30 dollars over the window, confirming the underlying fuel cost channel
Retail gasoline jumped roughly 0.59 dollars within 9 days, a clear input cost shock for road carriers
Transport ETF fell 2.6 sigma, corroborating the trucking and logistics sell-off
Transportation ETF dropped 3.6 sigma with a 9 percent peak decline, consistent with margin compression from fuel costs

us_airline_sector_put_options_spike_3f28.label: US airline sector put options spike
us_airline_sector_put_options_spike_3f28.description: Implied volatility on airline equity options surges as traders buy downside protection against further margin deterioration, with put skew steepening sharply in XAL-linked names.

evidence.note: Broad equity vol spiked over 6 sigma, which typically co-moves with sector-level implied vol though does not isolate airline-specific skew.
Crude surge of 20 sigma is the upstream margin shock that motivates airline-specific hedging demand.
Airline ETF fell nearly 5 sigma over the window, the kind of move that mechanically lifts realized and implied vol on sector options.

fed_rate_cut_expectations_repriced_lower.label: Fed rate cut expectations repriced lower
fed_rate_cut_expectations_repriced_lower.description: Rising breakeven inflation reduces the market's conviction that the Fed can cut rates, pushing fed funds futures to price fewer cuts in the near-term easing cycle.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose roughly 10bp at peak, over 2 sigma, confirming inflation repricing
10y breakeven moved less, suggesting front-loaded inflation concern rather than long-run de-anchoring
2y yield rose about 9bp, consistent with hawkish Fed repricing alongside breakevens
Fed funds futures price moved against cuts at over 2 sigma, directly evidencing repriced cut expectations

tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.label: TIPS real yield compression
tips_real_yield_compression_eec8bf.description: As nominal yields face flight-to-quality pressure while breakevens rise, the residual TIPS real yield compresses, reflecting stagflationary repricing of the real rate.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose roughly 2 sigma, peaking at 10bp above pre-event mean within 6 days, confirming inflation expectation repricing
10y TIPS real yield compressed 7bp, weaker move but consistent direction
5y TIPS real yield fell about 1.6 sigma, an 8bp compression that mirrors the breakeven expansion
TIP ETF rallied 0.6 percent, corroborating real yield compression

gold_price_rally_5560d9.label: Gold price rally
gold_price_rally_5560d9.description: Gold benefits from both the inflation hedge demand triggered by rising breakevens and concurrent safe-haven buying, pushing spot prices sharply higher.

evidence.note: 5y breakeven rose by roughly 2 sigma within 6 days, confirming the parent inflation expectations move.
10y breakeven moved modestly higher, weaker than 5y but directionally aligned.
10y real yield fell about 1 sigma, the precise channel through which higher breakevens support gold.
GLD rallied roughly 1.7 sigma with time-to-peak matching the real yield decline, consistent with the proposed mechanism.

russell_2000_underperformance_vs_spx_499.label: Russell 2000 underperformance vs SPX
russell_2000_underperformance_vs_spx_499.description: Small-cap equities lag large-cap as energy-cost sensitivity and tighter credit conditions hit domestically focused firms harder during the selloff.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, tightening credit conditions for small-cap balance sheets that rely more on floating-rate and HY debt.
Massive oil spike raises input costs, hitting small domestic firms harder due to weaker pricing power.
Vol spike consistent with forced de-risking that typically punishes higher-beta small-caps more.
Small-caps fell 4.5 sigma, materially deeper than SPY's 3.16 sigma drop, confirming small-cap underperformance.
Large-cap selloff was real but shallower than IWM, giving a clear relative underperformance gap.

us_equity_sector_rotation_to_defensives_.label: US equity sector rotation to defensives
us_equity_sector_rotation_to_defensives_.description: Utilities, consumer staples, and healthcare outperform cyclicals as institutional funds rotate within equities to reduce beta exposure during forced de-risking.

evidence.note: Severe vol shock confirms forced de-risking conditions
Utilities outperformed with positive deviation, consistent with defensive rotation
Staples declined modestly, partially contradicting the defensive rotation thesis
Consumer discretionary cyclicals fell nearly 2 sigma, consistent with rotation away from beta

clo_equity_tranche_price_drop_c11b06.label: CLO equity tranche price drop
clo_equity_tranche_price_drop_c11b06.description: CLO equity tranches, which absorb first losses on leveraged loan pools, reprice sharply lower as underlying loan NAVs decline and default expectations rise for energy-importing issuers.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, raising default expectations on leveraged loan pools and compressing CLO equity excess spread.
BBB OAS widened almost 3 sigma, reflecting broader credit stress consistent with CLO mezzanine and equity repricing.
Senior loan ETF fell over 2.5 sigma within 2 days, indicating loan price stress that flows directly into CLO collateral pools.
High yield credit sold off over 8 sigma, signaling severe credit risk repricing that hits CLO equity hardest as first-loss tranche.

us_leveraged_buyout_pipeline_freezes_0dd.label: US leveraged buyout pipeline freezes
us_leveraged_buyout_pipeline_freezes_0dd.description: Leveraged finance underwriters pull or reprice pending LBO bridge commitments as loan market pricing deteriorates, reducing new deal volumes sharply during the window.

evidence.note: HY OAS widened nearly 4 sigma, pushing new-issue clearing spreads well above typical bridge commitment flex caps of 100-150bp.
BBB OAS widened 2.8 sigma, confirming stress extends through the capital structure and tightens underwriting risk appetite.
Senior loan ETF fell 2.5 sigma within 2 days, indicating loan market repricing severe enough to invalidate prior underwriting flex assumptions.
High yield ETF dropped over 8 sigma, signaling broad leveraged finance distribution channel dysfunction that historically halts LBO syndication.

usd_investment_grade_issuance_freeze_e12.label: USD investment grade issuance freeze
usd_investment_grade_issuance_freeze_e12.description: Primary market IG corporate bond issuance stalls as the OAS surge makes new deals prohibitively expensive for investment grade issuers, reducing supply but signaling acute credit stress.

evidence.note: IG BBB OAS widened nearly 3 sigma immediately on the event, consistent with primary market conditions deteriorating sharply for IG issuers.
Moody's Baa spread widened about 2 sigma, reinforcing that IG borrowing costs jumped above pre-event hurdle rates.
LQD declined modestly, a weaker confirmation that secondary IG prices fell, which historically correlates with primary issuance pauses but is not a direct measure of issuance volume.

usd_short_end_funding_stress_rises_c2e5e.label: USD short-end funding stress rises
usd_short_end_funding_stress_rises_c2e5e.description: Elevated 2y UST yield and stagflation uncertainty push SOFR-OIS spreads and FRA-OIS basis wider as banks reprice short-term dollar funding risk.

evidence.note: 2y yield rose roughly 1.7 sigma, consistent with front-end repricing pressure
SOFR ticked up about 1.8 sigma above pre-event mean while DFF was unchanged, implying a mild widening of SOFR over fed funds
Fed funds effective unchanged, so the SOFR move reflects funding pressure rather than policy
Curve flattened by about 11 bps, confirming the parent move
T-bill ETF fell about 1.5 sigma, consistent with higher front-end yields

portfolio_impacts.summary: Broad equity drawdown led by transports, airlines, hotels, European and EM indices on oil-shock margin compression and risk-off flows; small-caps (IWM) underperform large-caps, with gold miners (GDX) the lone bright spot offsetting losses.

10y UST rallies on flight-to-quality and the 10y-2y curve flattens, but credit gets crushed: HY, IG, airline, EM sovereign and European peripheral spreads all widen sharply while mortgage rates and 2y yields rise on stagflation pricing — long duration Treasuries (TLT/IEF) win, credit (LQD/HYG/EMB) loses.
Gold rallies on combined safe-haven and inflation-hedge demand, supporting GLD and the broader DBC commodity basket; offset by EU carbon allowances (KRBN) declining on industrial demand destruction.

Fed funds futures (ZQ=F) reprice fewer cuts as breakevens rise, while airline-sector put-option demand spikes — net implication is tighter near-term policy expectations alongside elevated equity tail-risk hedging.

US household demand weakens via gasoline-price pass-through, falling confidence, rising unemployment expectations, slumping retail/auto sales, and a frozen LBO/IG primary market; EM central banks burn USD reserves to defend FX — broad stagflationary squeeze on consumer and credit cycles.

critique.counterargument: The WTI move in March-April 2011 is overwhelmingly attributable to the Libyan civil war and broader MENA/Arab Spring supply disruption, not Japanese substitution demand. Libya lost ~1.5 mb/d of light sweet crude exports starting late February 2011 — orders of magnitude larger than Japan's incremental crude/fuel oil burn for power, which IEA estimated at only ~200 kb/d. Brent moving 2.85 sigma vs WTI 2.1 sigma actually argues AGAINST the Japan channel: Japanese utilities buy Brent-linked and Mideast grades, not WTI, and the Brent-WTI spread blew out to record levels in 2011 due to Cushing/MENA factors unrelated to Japan. The supporting data cannot distinguish the Fukushima signal from the dominant Libya signal.

critique.counterargument: VIXCLS data does not exist for the 1990 Gulf War — the VIX index was launched in 1993 and the back-calculated series in FRED (VIXCLS) starts January 1990 but uses a methodology (1993 white paper, revised 2003) that was retrofitted, not contemporaneously traded. The cited 'pre-event 16.5 to post-event 24.6' move is real in the reconstructed series, but the supporting_data also shows SPY returned empty (SPY launched January 1993), confirming the window predates the actual instruments. Claiming sensitivity 0.85 / confidence 0.90 on a synthetic VIX series with no contemporaneous options market for the supply-shock channel is an overclaim on the evidence base.

rebuttal.rebuttal: The adversary is correct that VIX was not contemporaneously traded in 1990 and SPY did not yet exist, but the reconstructed VIXCLS series is computed from actual OEX/SPX options that DID trade in 1990 (CBOE listed S&P 100 options from 1983 and S&P 500 options from 1983), so the implied-volatility surge reflects real contemporaneous option premiums, not a synthetic backfill. The mechanism — investors bidding up downside protection on a geopolitical oil shock — is directly observable in the OEX options chain of August-October 1990, and the WTI move (DCOILWTICO) independently confirms the supply-shock channel. The adversary's point does justify a modest confidence haircut because the specific VIX methodology is retrofitted, but the underlying option-implied vol move is genuine and the edge survives.

critique.counterargument: Confidence 0.90 overstates the cleanliness of this channel. Major US carriers (DAL, UAL, AAL) typically hedge 20-50% of near-term fuel exposure, and a Hormuz closure would also crater air travel demand on Middle East/Asia routes and trigger broad risk-off equity selling — meaning the JETS selloff is partly demand-shock and risk-premium driven, not purely a fuel-margin mechanism. The named mechanism ('compressing margins on unhedged capacity') is therefore only one of three concurrent drivers, so attributing the full -4.7 sigma JETS move to it inflates the edge's specificity. Sensitivity 0.85 is defensible; confidence 0.90 should be ~0.70.

Attributing causality from WTI to broad USD via 'oil importer dollar demand' is a misidentified mechanism — EUR weakness reflects Europe's gas (TTF) and geopolitical exposure, not WTI-driven petrodollar flows.

The current regime is the opposite: a commodity-driven supply shock with 5y5y breakevens (T5YIFR) jumping, which structurally inverts the flight-to-quality reflex because the Fed is forced to hike into the volatility, not cut. critique.counterargument: VIX-to-gold is not a reliable channel. In the March 2020 COVID crash, VIX hit 82 (highest ever) yet gold sold off ~12% from March 9-19 as investors liquidated gold to meet margin calls and raise USD. Similarly in October 2008, VIX spiked above 80 while gold fell from ~$900 to ~$720. The cited co-movement at day 11 likely reflects a common driver (geopolitical shock → both VIX and gold) rather than VIX→gold causation; in pure financial-stress VIX spikes without a geopolitical/inflation overlay, gold often falls. A 0.75 sensitivity overstates a relationship that flips sign in liquidity crises.

rebuttal.rebuttal: The adversary is right that TRY is contaminated by idiosyncratic CBRT policy, but the link does not depend on TRY. The CEW ETF moved 8 sigma and EEM moved 7 sigma — these are broad baskets spanning Asia, LatAm, and EMEA, not TRY-driven. critique.counterargument: Energy-led CPI spikes historically do NOT reliably push 10y yields up — they often do the opposite via growth-fear/recession pricing. In mid-2008, headline CPI surged to 5.6% on an oil shock yet 10y UST yields FELL from ~4.1% in June to ~3.8% by August as markets priced demand destruction. Same pattern in 2022 H2: oil rolled over while yields kept rising on core/policy, breaking the energy-CPI→10y link. The mechanism conflates headline with breakevens, but 5y5y forward inflation expectations (T5YIFR) are notoriously well-anchored to energy moves — the cited series even returned empty, undermining the breakeven leg of the claim.

critique.counterargument: Empirically, breakevens respond much more to oil prices directly than to the CPI energy print itself, because the CPI release is lagged and largely anticipated by the time it lands. Studies (e.g., Hammoudeh & Reboredo; Fed staff notes) show TIPS breakevens co-move contemporaneously with WTI/Brent at near-daily frequency, while the marginal reaction to a CPI energy subindex surprise—conditional on already-known oil moves—is small and often statistically insignificant. The edge as written posits CPI energy → breakevens, but the causal arrow in markets runs oil → breakevens, with CPI energy being a downstream accounting consequence. Compounding this, every supporting series (T5YIE, T10YIE, T5YIFR, TIP) returned empty, so the 0.25/0.25 scores rest on zero direct evidence of the claimed transmission.

critique.counterargument: The causal arrow is reversed: airline equities selling off is a consequence of higher jet fuel costs, not a driver of crack spreads. Airlines do not bid up physical kerosene cracks by selling stock; refiners set cracks based on distillate inventories, refinery utilization, and wholesale demand from jobbers/airlines on contracted volumes. Equity tape moves on minutes; crack spreads move on physical product balances and refinery yields. The named mechanism ('airlines scrambling on thin hedge books') would operate via the jet fuel physical/swap market directly from the crude shock, making the equity selloff a coincident symptom rather than a cause.

That is an empirical regime claim, not an accounting one — in a non-stagflationary regime, rising breakevens typically push nominal yields up and leave real yields roughly unchanged (e.g., 2016-2018 reflation episodes where DFII10 was stable while T10YIE rose). The edge does carry incremental information by specifying which leg of the identity absorbs the shock, though I concede some downweighting is warranted since part of the move is mechanical.

critique.counterargument: The causal arrow is likely reversed or simultaneous, not skew-causes-selloff. Put skew steepening is overwhelmingly a *response* to spot declines and rising realized vol, not a driver of them — dealers are typically long skew via structured product flows, and skew steepening in a selloff reflects demand for puts after price already moved. The named mechanism (dealer delta hedging from skew) is also second-order relative to the vol-target/risk-parity deleveraging, which is already captured by the VIX→selloff edge elsewhere, making this edge largely redundant.

rebuttal.rebuttal: The adversary correctly notes that CLO indenture OC tests use par (not market) values and that CLO equity secondary marks lag — but the transmission still operates through two intact channels. First, CLO equity cash flows (not just marks) compress in real time when underlying loans experience credit migration to CCC: most indentures cap CCC holdings at 7.5%, and excess CCC is haircut to market value for OC purposes, so loan price declines DO feed OC tests via the CCC bucket. Second, in March 2020 CLO equity BWIC prices dropped 30-50% within 3-4 weeks of the loan selloff (not 'months'), and primary CLO equity issuance shut entirely — both observable repricing events. critique.counterargument: The 2022 leveraged loan selloff is a direct counter-example: BKLN fell sharply and HY OAS widened materially through 2022, yet the LBO pipeline did not 'freeze' — instead underwriters ate the losses on hung deals (Twitter/X, Citrix, Nielsen) and continued originating, with banks holding paper on balance sheet rather than halting origination. The mechanism described conflates 'syndication difficulty' with 'pipeline freeze'; bridge commitments are contractually binding and underwriters typically flex pricing/structure or warehouse rather than pull. critique.counterargument: The mechanism conflates local-currency debt servicing with hard-currency sovereign spreads, but USD-denominated sovereign debt is serviced in dollars, not local currency, so FX depreciation does not mechanically raise the issuer's USD coupon burden. The real channel is fiscal capacity (ability to source USD via exports/reserves), which for commodity-importing EMs is hit by terms-of-trade, not FX per se. Additionally, EM FX and EMBI spreads are largely co-determined by the same global risk/dollar factor (DXY, UST yields), so attributing spread widening to FX depreciation risks double-counting the common driver already cited (DTWEXBGS). The 0.80 confidence overstates a partly spurious causal link.

However, the edge survives via a distinct pathway the adversary actually names: FX depreciation is a real-time signal of deteriorating USD fiscal capacity (reserve drawdown, weaker terms of trade pass-through, capital outflow pressure), which directly drives hard-currency sovereign spreads. critique.counterargument: The link conflates EM FX weakness with EM equity drawdowns, but the relationship is far from monotonic and has visible counter-examples. In 2016 H2, broad USD strengthened sharply (DTWEXBGS rose post-election) and several EM currencies (TRY, MXN) weakened, yet MSCI EM equities held up and finished the year positive as commodity exporters rallied. critique.counterargument: Post-2015 EM central bank doctrine has shifted away from heavy spot FX intervention toward letting currencies float while using rate hikes and FX swap/forward instruments (notably the RBI and Banco de Mexico) to manage volatility. The TRY case is the cleanest counter-example: through 2021-2023 Turkey's lira collapsed multiple sigma without a corresponding drawdown in gross reserves — the CBRT used swap lines and back-door state-bank intervention, and at times reserves rose during depreciation episodes. India under the RBI typically intervenes in NDF and forward markets, which does not show up as a gross reserve drawdown in IMF COFER. So depreciation → gross reserve drawdown is a weak, regime-dependent link, and the agent's own 0.35/0.40 scoring already concedes this.
